description:
Hi team,

Here are updates to apply to the website:

1. Homepage CTA Updates
Residential Section:
  • Replace the current “Read More” button with:
    👉 Start Your Home Project
Commercial Section:
  • Replace the current “Read More” button with:
    👉 Start Your Commercial Project

2. Residential Design Landing Page Update
Page currently begins with “Residential Design from Eden Oaks.”
Insert a new short intro section directly below the headline and above the paragraph that starts with “Here at Eden Oaks…”
Add the following:
Ready to Get Started?
Share a few details about your project, and our design team will follow up to schedule your free consultation.
[Start My Project] → link this button to the same quote form currently used for “GET A QUOTE” farther down the page.
Keep all existing content on the page (paragraphs, bullet points, quote/contact buttons, and image slider) as is.

3. Commercial Millwork Landing Page Update
Page currently begins with “Commercial Millwork and Design in Colorado.”
Insert a new short intro section directly below the headline and above the paragraph that starts with “The team at Eden Oaks…”
Add the following:
Have a Project in Mind?
Send us your drawings or ideas and our commercial team will provide a bid and walk you through next steps.
[Start My Commercial Project] → link this button to the same quote form used for residential inquiries.
Keep the rest of the content (service descriptions, links, and categories) as is.

4. Styling Notes:
  • Match button styling (color, font, size) to other CTAs on the site for consistency.
  • Add light spacing above the new intro sections so they visually separate from the headline.

5. Quote Form Updates
Is it possible to update our quote form so customers can upload supporting files like drawings, blueprints, or inspiration photos when submitting project inquiries?
Here’s what I’m envisioning:
1. Add file upload capability
  • Allow uploads for images (JPG, PNG) and PDFs.
  • Limit total file size to around 20MB (or whatever is manageable with our system).
  • Label the field:
    “Upload drawings, inspiration photos, or project files (optional)”
2. How uploads are handled
Please confirm how the uploaded files will be delivered to us:
  • Option 1: Files come through as attachments on the email notification (preferred, if possible).
  • Option 2: Files are stored in the backend or cloud, and we receive a link to access them.
If we’ll need to log in to view files, please make sure the process is simple and that we know exactly where to find them.
3. Other details
  • Make sure the uploads work smoothly on both desktop and mobile.
  • Ensure file storage is secure (no executable files allowed).
  • Match the new field’s style and spacing to the rest of the form.
If it’s easy to do, a simple progress bar or confirmation message after upload would be a nice touch.
The goal is to make it easier for customers — especially commercial and residential design clients — to share drawings or inspiration images upfront.

Let me know if you have any questions. Thank you!

description:
Hi there -

Brian would like a new page created to showcase some clients he has worked with over the years.
Right now we only have 1 featured client and a story, but will be adding more.
Will you please feature this story and photo?
The tab on the main page can be called 'Funny Client Stories'.

Thank you,

Jessica Cartwright
719-301-0924

Let me introduce to you my very 1st clients from over 25 years ago, JC and Heather. We have kept in touch all these years! Relationships are so important to me and my team.

 

JC and Heather have been in the same house I sold them years ago, and while there have been discussions about possible moves, they have loved their house and updated it beautifully.

Well one upcoming April Fool’s Day, I received a very interesting call from Heather. She asked if she could borrow one of my real estate signs, which I was happy to lend her, but of course, had to ask ‘why?’. She wanted to get a rise out of JC when he returned home that April Fool’s and saw a ‘for sale’ sign in their yard! I loved the idea and said, ‘I’m in’, sending her home with one of my signs.

 

Heather and her devious daughter put the sign in the ground while JC and their son were away for the day, waiting anxiously for them to come home. When JC and his son arrived home and saw the sign, they were taken aback. JC’s son was in complete shock and said to his dad, “I didn’t know you and mom were planning on selling the house”.

 

To prank Heather in return JC said, “I have an idea. Two can play this game” and he called one of his neighbors. He asked his neighbor if he would call Heather to make an offer on their house, and the neighbor happily made the call, at which point Heather had to backpeddle to get out of the mess because they definitely didn’t want to sell their house! This is the only time a real estate sign has been on that house since they became my first clients and we built a friendship. Whether it’s a prank or a real sale, I’m so glad they chose me to help!

 

Love the character and humor of the clients that I work with! Would love to have the opportunity to get to know you and develop a 25+ year history with you and your family as well.

name: #20693 Quandary: BreadcrumbList JSON-LD
description:
Hey April - per my pervious email, I also received notification that we had the following:


I have a medium severity content issue on https://quandarycg.com/careers

ssue: BreadcrumbList schema is missing Description: 

Add BreadcrumbList schema markup to your page with the following properties: 

BreadcrumbList structured data (JSON-LD, Microdata, or RDFa) with ≥2 items. 



BELOW IS THE EXAMPLE OF THE RECOMMENDED actionable steps to fix this issue. IS THIS SOMETHING YOU ALL CAN FIX PLEASE?
THANK YOU!


Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to the careers page, with at least 2 breadcrumb items, then make sure it’s included in the page HTML so crawlers can read it.

Use this as a simple pattern:

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
  "itemListElement": [
    {
      "@type": "ListItem",
      "position": 1,
      "name": "Home",
      "item": "https://quandarycg.com/"
    },
    {
      "@type": "ListItem",
      "position": 2,
      "name": "Careers",
      "item": "https://quandarycg.com/careers"
    }
  ]
}
</script>

After adding it, verify the page source contains the script and test the URL in Google’s Rich Results Test or Schema Markup Validator.

name: #20334 Website Updates - Sandy Scott LLC
description:
Hi, 

The site looks great! After reviewing, we would like to add a couple more updates to the website. Please see the instructions below and don't hesitate to reach out with any questions. 

Strategic Advisory Session Pop-Up

We need a pop-up experience added to sandyscottllc.com to promote a new offering (Strategic Advisory Sessions) to previous coaching clients visiting the site. Below are the requirements.

1. Format
  • Bottom-right slide-in — not a centered modal/overlay. It should feel like a gentle notification, not an interruption. Small, clean, professional. Consistent with the site's existing visual style and brand. Trigger after 4 seconds on the page.
2. Two-Step Flow
  • Step 1 — The slide-in:
    • Headline: "Worked with Sandy before?"
    • Subtext: "She's now offering on-demand Strategic Advisory Sessions for physician executives."
    • Two buttons:
    • → "Yes, tell me more" (advances to Step 2)
    • → "Not yet" (dismisses the slide-in)
  • Step 2 — Expanded view (same slide-in expands or transitions):
    • Headline: "Welcome back."
    • Body: "Book a focused, 60-minute strategic conversation with Sandy — confidential, on-demand, built for physician executives navigating high-stakes decisions."
    • Subtext: "Limited to 3 sessions per month. Alumni launch rate: $397."
    • Button: "Book a Session →" — links to: https://calendly.com/sandyscott/strategic-advisory-session
    • Include a small ✕ or "Close" option to dismiss.
3. Sticky Bar (separate from the pop-up)
  • In addition to the slide-in, add a persistent sticky bar across the top or bottom of the site. This is always visible (not a pop-up, doesn't need to be dismissed). Keep it subtle — one line.
  • Copy: "Physician executives who've worked with Sandy: Book a Strategic Advisory Session →"
  • The → links to: https://calendly.com/sandyscott/strategic-advisory-sessio
4. Behavior Rules
  • If a visitor clicks "Not yet" on the slide-in, suppress the slide-in for 7 days
  • None of this should interfere with or replace the existing Calendly embed at the bottom of the site — that stays as-is
5. Mobile
  • The slide-in should be responsive and thumb-friendly on mobile
  • Button tap targets should be at least 48px height
  • The sticky bar should be a thin bottom banner on mobile (not top — it'll interfere with navigation)
Take care,
